computation_name: Filtered ANN with Attribute and Range Constraints
definition: Build a vector index that applies metadata, categorical, temporal, spatial, or numeric range filters during nearest-neighbor search rather than treating filtering as a separate post-processing step. Use it when the scientific audit needs train neighbors inside a matched cohort, site, time window, assay, tissue, population, or covariate slice.

scale_profile.best_for: Large embedding audits where each eval sample should only compare against eligible train samples under scientific or data-governance constraints.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of vectors depending on filter selectivity, index family, and memory budget.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Filters are trivial, highly selective enough for exact search, or scientifically inappropriate because they hide the novelty axis being audited.

tunable_parameters.name: filter_predicate
tunable_parameters.purpose: Declares the metadata, range, temporal, or cohort constraint applied before or during search.

tunable_parameters.name: k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of eligible train neighbors retained per evaluation sample.

tunable_parameters.name: filter_execution_mode
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls pre-filter, post-filter, hybrid, or integrated filtered-index search.

tunable_parameters.name: search_budget
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls recall and latency under selective filters.

quality_gates: Report the exact filter predicate and justify why it belongs in the similarity computation rather than the novelty axis.
Measure recall@k against exact filtered search on representative slices, including rare filters.
Check that filter selectivity does not leave eval samples with too few train neighbors for stable SPECTRA bins.
failure_modes: Post-filtering ordinary ANN results can miss eligible nearest neighbors under selective filters.
Filtering on a variable that should be audited can remove the generalization signal.
Rare attribute combinations can produce unstable distances or empty candidate sets.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py as an exact reference after applying the same eligibility predicate; production filtered ANN should emit the same SPECTRA columns and include the filter predicate.

citations.title: UNIFY: A Unified Index for Range Filtered Approximate Nearest Neighbors Search
citations.evidence: Studies unified indexing for high-dimensional ANN queries with range filters rather than relying only on pre-filter or post-filter strategies.

citations.title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search with Window Filters
citations.evidence: Formulates ANN search with label-window constraints, a direct analogue of filtered train-neighbor retrieval for SPECTRA audits.
